title: The Dock and Launching Apps
description: Learn to open and close apps using the Dock.

steps.instruction: The Dock is the bar of icons at the bottom of your screen. Click the Finder icon (📁) to open Finder.
steps.hint: Look at the bottom of the screen for the row of app icons. Click the folder icon (📁) which is Finder.

steps.instruction: Now click the TextEdit icon (📝) in the Dock to open a text editor.
steps.hint: Find the notepad icon (📝) in the Dock and click it to open TextEdit.

steps.instruction: Notice that both apps show a small dot below their Dock icon — this means they're running. Click on the Finder window to bring it to the front.
steps.hint: Click anywhere on the Finder window to bring it in front of the TextEdit window.

steps.instruction: Close the TextEdit window by clicking its red traffic light button (top-left corner).
steps.hint: Click the red circle in the top-left of the TextEdit window.

steps.instruction: Open the Terminal app (⬛) from the Dock.
steps.hint: Find the black square icon (⬛) in the Dock and click it.

steps.instruction: Now open System Settings (⚙️) from the Dock.
steps.hint: Find the gear icon (⚙️) in the Dock and click it.
